expectations.evidence: components/ReviewCard.tsx exists in outputs directory and contains a complete component implementation (100 lines).
File was created as components/ReviewCard.tsx (flat file), not components/ReviewCard/index.tsx. Transcript confirms: 'Created: components/ReviewCard.tsx'.
The file is a flat ReviewCard.tsx. Transcript Step 4 explicitly states 'File: components/ReviewCard.tsx'. No folder structure was used.
Component is named 'ReviewCard' (PascalCase) as seen in 'export default function ReviewCard(...)' on line 14.
Line 3: 'export interface ReviewCardProps { ... }' — follows the {ComponentName}Props naming convention.
Lines 36-49: Avatar section with data-testid='review-card-avatar', includes img element for URL and initials fallback div.
Lines 59-79: Star rating section with 5 SVG stars, filled amber for active (i < rating) and zinc for inactive, with aria-label for accessibility.

expectations.text: useEffect, fetch, axios, useQuery, useMutation 호출이 없다
expectations.evidence: Searched the entire ReviewCard.tsx output — none of these forbidden patterns appear. The component is purely presentational.
No folder was created. The component is a flat file at components/ReviewCard.tsx, not inside a reviewCard/ directory.
File is at components/ReviewCard.tsx, directly under components/. Not placed under components/common/ or any domain subfolder.

claims.claim: The component follows existing project conventions observed from Button.tsx, ProductCard/index.tsx, StatsCard.tsx
claims.evidence: The transcript notes ProductCard/index.tsx uses a folder/index.tsx pattern, yet the executor created a flat file (ReviewCard.tsx). This contradicts the stated convention discovery.
Transcript Step 6 states tsc --noEmit found only pre-existing errors, none in ReviewCard.tsx.

claims.claim: Linter auto-fixed the ReviewCard component (STAR_PATH constant extraction)
claims.evidence: The output file shows STAR_PATH as a top-level constant (line 11), consistent with the transcript's claim of auto-fix.

claims.claim: Dark mode support via dark: variants
claims.evidence: Multiple dark: Tailwind classes present: dark:border-zinc-800, dark:bg-zinc-900, dark:text-zinc-50, dark:text-zinc-300, dark:text-zinc-600, dark:text-zinc-500.

suggestions.reason: Assertions 2, 3, and 12 all test overlapping aspects of the same convention (folder structure). They collectively catch the same failure. Consider consolidating into a single assertion that checks the full path pattern 'components/{domain}/reviewCard/index.tsx'.

No assertion checks whether the component uses 'use client' directive, which the transcript identified as a project convention and which is present in the output. This is a convention-adherence signal worth testing.
No assertion verifies that the showcase page was updated with sample usage of the component. The showcase_page.tsx output shows integration, but no expectation covers it.

eval_feedback.overall: The assertions cover content features well but have redundancy in folder-structure checks (3 assertions testing the same convention). The biggest gap is no assertion for 'use client' directive adherence despite it being a stated project convention.
